title: Meletis and Durscheid (2022): Writing Systems and Their Use
subtitle: A review of a major comparative writing theory and its typological limit with signed language writing
summary: Review essay on Dimitrios Meletis and Christa Durscheid's 2022 grapholinguistic monograph Writing Systems and Their Use.
synopsis: This review treats Writing Systems and Their Use as one of the strongest recent comparative theories of writing. It argues that the book meaningfully advances grapholinguistics by rejecting crude speech-dependence, giving writing internal structure, and taking visual form and typological self-critique seriously, while also arguing that the framework still appears typologically incomplete once signed language writing and SignWriting are treated as real writing on their own terms.
